companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Ultra, an open model for long-running agentic work, as a founding contribution to the Nemotron Coalition. Annual Meeting proxy results (Form 8-K) are due tomorrow (June 30). Vera Rubin runs 81% of TOP500 supercomputers and powers 35 European HPC systems. BioNeMo Agent Toolkit is live for life sciences AI pipelines.

items.headline: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Ultra open model for long-running agents via Nemotron Coalition
items.detail: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Ultra, an open-weights model built for extended agentic reasoning and long-horizon tasks, as a founding contribution to the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. The coalition brings together Mistral AI and other open frontier model builders to advance open-source alternatives to closed frontier models. Nemotron 3 Ultra joins NVIDIA's broader platform push that includes BioNeMo, DSX, and ISC Hamburg deployments.

items.headline: NVIDIA Annual Meeting proxy results (Form 8-K) due tomorrow June 30; FY2026 revenue $215.9B (+65% YoY)
items.detail: NVIDIA held its 2026 Annual Meeting online on June 24. Stockholders voted on 10 director elections, executive compensation, PwC ratification for fiscal 2027, and four shareholder proposals. Final voting results will be filed in a Form 8-K by tomorrow (June 30). FY2026 highlights from the proxy: revenue $215.9 billion (+65% YoY), gross margin 71.1%, operating income $130.4 billion, diluted EPS $4.90 (+67%).

items.headline: BioNeMo Agent Toolkit launched at ISC Hamburg; NVIDIA runs 81% of TOP500; 35 European Vera Rubin HPC systems
items.detail: At ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg, NVIDIA launched the BioNeMo Agent Toolkit enabling AI agents to gather evidence, reason across findings, run computational experiments, and accelerate scientific discovery. NVIDIA technology runs 81% of the TOP500 supercomputers, with 90% of new entries powered by NVIDIA hardware. A record 35 AI HPC systems using the Vera Rubin platform are in development across European research institutions.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro's June 30 deadline is tomorrow at roughly 50% prediction market odds, with insider reporting pointing to a July slip — Google's first publicly missed model window this year. Gemini Managed Agents API launched in public preview with the Antigravity Agent. Five senior researchers departed in one week (John Jumper, Noam Shazeer, Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, Arthur Conmy), erasing ~$270B in Alphabet market cap.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro June 30 deadline is tomorrow; July delay widely reported; ~50% prediction market odds
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro — Google's next frontier model with a self-imposed June 30 launch window — sits at approximately 50% odds in prediction markets as of June 29. CryptoBriefing and Analytics Insight both report insider signals pointing to a July slip, which would be Google DeepMind's first publicly missed model window in 2026. Expected specs remain: 2M token context, Deep Think reasoning, multimodal frontier performance, priced at approximately $15 per million input tokens.

items.headline: Gemini Managed Agents API in public preview; Antigravity Agent launched for autonomous agentic tasks
items.detail: Google launched Managed Agents in the Gemini API in public preview, enabling developers to build and deploy stateful autonomous agents running in secure, isolated Google-hosted Linux sandbox environments. The Antigravity Agent — Google's first managed agent — can autonomously plan, reason, write and execute code, manage files, and browse the web inside its sandbox. Gemini 3.5 Flash remains available for agents and coding ahead of the Pro full launch.

items.headline: Five senior DeepMind researchers depart in one week — Alphabet loses ~$270B in market cap
items.detail: Google DeepMind's worst talent week of 2026: Nobel Prize winner John Jumper (AlphaFold2 co-creator, to Anthropic), Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer (reportedly to OpenAI), and researchers Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, and Arthur Conmy (all to Anthropic) departed within a single week. Alphabet's stock fell roughly 7% across two trading sessions, erasing approximately $270 billion in market capitalization. The talent losses add delivery risk to Apple's Gemini-powered Siri dependency.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Copilot Cowork Frontier grace period ends July 1 — 2 days away. Microsoft published its third annual AI in Education Report (June 24) showing widespread adoption across K-12 and higher education. MAI-Code-1, MAI-Code-1-Flash, and MAI-Thinking-1 (35B) launched at Build 2026. Majorana 2 quantum chip achieves 20-second average qubit lifetime and 1,000x reliability improvement.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork billing activates July 1 (2 days away); Microsoft IQ and Work IQ APIs GA
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork reached general availability June 16 under Copilot Credits usage-based billing. Frontier preview tenants have until July 1 — now 2 days away — before billing activates and access is cut off for unconfigured organizations. Microsoft IQ (Work IQ APIs + Web IQ) is GA across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio. Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium with Copilot formalize as permanent commercial SKUs on July 1.

items.headline: Microsoft AI in Education Report: widespread adoption across K-12 and higher ed; new AI teaching tools at no cost
items.detail: Microsoft released its third annual AI in Education Report on June 24, revealing strong momentum behind AI adoption in K-12 and higher education, alongside increasing demand for implementation support. Microsoft also announced a new wave of AI-powered teaching and learning experiences — available at no additional cost — designed to improve learning outcomes and student engagement. The report accompanies Microsoft's broader push to integrate MAI models and Copilot into education workflows.

items.headline: MAI-Code-1 and MAI-Code-1-Flash at Build 2026; MAI-Thinking-1 (35B) for enterprise agents; Majorana 2 quantum chip
items.detail: Microsoft unveiled MAI-Code-1 — its inaugural code-generation model from written descriptions, tuned for GitHub and VS Code — alongside MAI-Code-1-Flash (faster, lower cost) and MAI-Thinking-1 (35B parameters, enterprise agentic reasoning). Seven new MAI models total were announced at Build 2026. Majorana 2 achieves a 20-second average qubit lifetime with instances up to one minute — a 1,000x reliability improvement. Azure Cobalt 200 VMs deliver 50% performance improvement for modern agentic AI workloads.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna launched June 26 in limited preview under U.S. government-imposed access restrictions; GPT-4.5 retired the same day. OpenAI acquired Ona (AI research and agentic workflows) on June 21. Confidential S-1 filed June 8 with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan; company leaning toward a 2027 IPO. ChatGPT global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% — first time below 50%.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna launched June 26 under government restrictions; GPT-4.5 retired
items.detail: OpenAI launched three GPT-5.6 models on June 26 in limited preview under U.S. government-imposed access restrictions: Sol (flagship; $5/$30 per million input/output tokens), Terra (balanced; $2.50/$15), and Luna (fast; $1/$6). Sol targets frontier reasoning and long-horizon agentic work; Terra matches GPT-5.5 performance at 2x lower cost; Luna is the fastest and most affordable. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26, replaced by GPT-5.5 in existing conversations. OpenAI hopes to make GPT-5.6 widely available 'in the coming weeks.'

items.headline: OpenAI acquires Ona (AI research and agentic work) on June 21; Codex Remote GA on all ChatGPT plans
items.detail: OpenAI acquired Ona on June 21, 2026, an AI company specializing in research and agentic workflow capabilities. Codex Remote is now generally available on all ChatGPT plans, allowing users to start or continue work on a connected Mac or Windows host from the mobile app, review progress, and approve actions remotely. Memory controls were updated: users can now delete specific individual memories and turn memory off entirely via a single action.

items.headline: IPO leaning 2027; confidential S-1 filed June 8; 42-state AG probe; ChatGPT market share 46.4%
items.detail: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 8 with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. A coalition of 42 state AGs (led by NY AG Letitia James) opened a formal probe June 12 covering user harm and advertising practices. Sensor Tower found ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share at 46.4% — first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Fable 5 remains suspended Day 17 as of June 29; Axios reports the Trump administration is close to allowing Fable access 'as soon as this coming week,' pending Pentagon and NSA sign-off. Mythos 5 was partially restored June 26 to ~100 companies and federal agencies. AI for Science virtual event is tomorrow (June 30, 10 AM PST) featuring Nobel laureate John Jumper. Anthropic is tracking toward its first operating profit (~$559M) in Q2 2026 at a $47B annualized revenue run rate. Series H ($30B+) closing at a >$900B valuation; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target.

items.headline: Fable 5 suspended Day 17; Mythos 5 partially restored June 26 to ~100 orgs; broad restoration 'as soon as this coming week'
items.detail: Fable 5 remains suspended for all general users — consumers, API developers, Claude Code, and international subscribers — on Day 17 as of June 29. Mythos 5 was partially restored June 26 under a Commerce Secretary Lutnick letter, permitting approximately 100 trusted companies and federal agencies including critical infrastructure operators to access it. Axios reported June 27 that the Trump administration is close to allowing Fable 5 access 'as soon as this coming week,' pending Pentagon and NSA sign-off.

items.headline: AI for Science event tomorrow (June 30); John Jumper joins from DeepMind; $47B ARR; first operating profit ~$559M Q2
items.detail: Nobel laureate John Jumper — 2024 Chemistry Nobel Prize winner and AlphaFold2 co-creator — joins Anthropic from Google DeepMind and will appear first at 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event tomorrow (June 30, 10 AM PST). Anthropic is tracking toward its first operating profit of approximately $559 million in Q2 2026 at an annualized revenue run rate of $47 billion — representing roughly 1,400% year-over-year growth. The Series H round exceeding $30 billion targets a valuation above $900 billion.

items.headline: Seoul office opened June 17; TCS partnership June 12 for regulated industries; largest distillation attack identified
items.detail: Anthropic opened its Seoul office on June 17, 2026, and announced new partnerships across the Korean AI ecosystem. Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and Anthropic partnered on June 12 to bring Claude to regulated industries including financial services, healthcare, and government. Separately, Anthropic accused Alibaba-linked Qwen lab operators of executing its largest-ever identified distillation attack: approximately 25,000 fraudulent accounts conducted 28.8 million Claude exchanges between April and June 2026, apparently to train competing models on Claude's outputs.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta launched Meta One, a paid AI subscription ($7.99/month Plus, $19.99/month Premium), as a revenue diversification move. Meta is building Arena, a Llama-powered prediction market app (codenames: Antwerp/FBForecast). Meta Glasses launched June 23 with strong early demand. A June 26 CNBC analysis found Meta's flurry of June AI initiatives has not helped lift the stock. More than 1,600 employees signed a petition against AI monitoring of keystrokes and clicks.

items.headline: Meta One paid AI subscription announced: $7.99/month Plus, $19.99/month Premium
items.detail: Meta announced Meta One, a paid AI subscription offering extra features across the Family of Apps as a revenue diversification play. Meta One Plus costs $7.99 per month and Meta One Premium costs $19.99 per month. The offering targets power users who want enhanced capabilities on WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and Threads — positioning Meta AI as a competitor to ChatGPT Plus and Claude Pro in the consumer AI subscription market.

items.headline: Meta building Arena AI prediction market app powered by Llama; codenames Antwerp and FBForecast
items.detail: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed a team to build a standalone prediction market app called Arena (internal codenames: 'Antwerp' and 'FBForecast') that uses Llama to automatically generate questions from trending topics, personalize market recommendations, and resolve markets via AI. The app competes with Kalshi, Polymarket, and Manifold. No public launch date has been announced. At Cannes Lions 2026, Meta also announced an end-to-end AI creative solution for marketers and unified creator partnership tools.

items.headline: Meta Glasses launched June 23 ($299–$399); strong early demand; AI Mode live on Facebook; capex $115–135B unchanged
items.detail: Meta's in-house Meta Glasses (launched June 23 with EssilorLuxottica at $299–$399) show strong early sales with smart glasses shipments up 167% YoY in Q1 2026 and Meta at 69.2% market share. AI Mode launched on Facebook, pulling from public posts across Groups and Reels via a three-tier model routing system. Meta's 2026 capex guidance of $115–135 billion remains unchanged. More than 1,600 Meta employees signed a petition against a program monitoring clicks and keystrokes for AI training data.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: Amazon announced a $13 billion investment to expand AI and cloud infrastructure in India (Mumbai and Hyderabad). Amazon Quick — an AI assistant for work with desktop app — launched, alongside AWS WAF's new AI traffic monetization capability. Bedrock AgentCore is SOC 1/2/3 compliant with failure, intent, and trajectory insights. G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell deliver 4.6x AI inference performance. Amazon eyeing a $3 trillion valuation.

items.headline: Amazon announces $13 billion India AI and cloud infrastructure investment (Mumbai and Hyderabad)
items.detail: Amazon announced plans to invest an additional $13 billion to expand AI and cloud infrastructure in India, expanding AWS data center capacity in Mumbai and Hyderabad to deliver custom AI chips, managed AI services, and developer tools. The investment reinforces AWS's position as a global infrastructure platform for AI workloads and follows large capital commitments in the US, Europe, and Japan throughout 2025–2026.

items.headline: Amazon Quick AI assistant for work launches; AWS WAF adds AI traffic monetization capability
items.detail: AWS launched Amazon Quick — an AI assistant for work with a desktop app and expanded integrations — enabling employees to search internal knowledge, conduct multi-step research, and work with AI models in a unified platform. AWS WAF added a new AI traffic monetization capability, enabling content providers to price, meter, and collect payment from AI bots and agents accessing their content and APIs. AWS also expanded Amazon Connect into four agentic AI solutions covering supply chain, hiring, customer experience, and healthcare.

items.headline: Bedrock AgentCore SOC 1/2/3 compliant; G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 deliver 4.6x inference; Grok 4.3 on Bedrock
items.detail: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore received an optimization update adding failure insights, intent insights, and trajectory insights for production agent improvement. The service is now SOC 1, 2, and 3 compliant. AWS became the first major cloud provider to support NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs via new G7 instances, delivering up to 4.6x AI inference performance vs G6. Grok 4.3 is available on Amazon Bedrock with tool calling, structured output, and response streaming.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: Grok 4.5 (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 model, trained on Cursor data) is in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. SPCX trades at approximately $153 — still 32% below its June 16 ATH of $225.64. Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 — 2 days away. Grok adult content exceeds 10 billion images monthly; xAI engineers acknowledge CSAM has no current technical fix. The $60B Cursor acquisition remains on track for Q3 2026 close.

items.headline: Grok 4.5 in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX — 1.5T parameters, V9 model, trained on Cursor coding data
items.detail: xAI's Grok 4.5 is running in private beta inside Tesla and SpaceX as of late June 2026. The model is built on xAI's V9 foundation (1.5 trillion parameters, training completed May 26) and incorporates supplemental training on Cursor AI coding data — integrating assets from the $60 billion pending acquisition ahead of its formal Q3 2026 close. Early evaluations place performance close to or above Opus. Monthly launch cadence planned.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 (2 days away); both Colossus facilities fully committed
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion) starts July 1 — now 2 days away — at $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access via Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Both Colossus 1 (rented to Anthropic at ~$1.25B/month) and Colossus 2 (Reflection AI) are now fully committed to third parties.

items.headline: Grok adult content tops 10B images/month; xAI engineers admit CSAM has no current technical fix
items.detail: Grok's adult content generation is producing more than 10 billion images per month, with SpaceX's IPO prospectus setting aside $530 million to cover potential related litigation. xAI engineers publicly acknowledged that CSAM generation is an unsolved technical problem with no current fix, drawing criticism from child safety advocates and lawmakers. This represents a regulatory and reputational risk for SpaceX ahead of the Cursor acquisition close.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator, built in nine months for LLM inference, targeting H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Q2 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8B (+143% YoY); FY2026 full-year guidance is $56B; FY2027 target is $100B+. The $0.65/share quarterly dividend is payable tomorrow (June 30). The $35B Apollo/Blackstone AI XPV Platform is operational, enabling 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute through 2028.

items.headline: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil Jalapeño — first custom OpenAI ASIC built in 9 months; H2 2026 Stargate target
items.detail: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator chip — co-developed from initial design to manufacturing tape-out in nine months, what the companies describe as the fastest ASIC development cycle ever achieved in high-performance advanced semiconductors. Jalapeño is a reticle-sized ASIC designed from the ground up for LLM inference, offering substantially better performance per watt than current state-of-the-art. Initial deployment targets H2 2026 in Stargate data centers.

items.headline: Q2 AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY); FY26 guidance $56B; FY27 target $100B+; dividend payable tomorrow June 30
items.detail: Broadcom reported Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) on record quarterly total revenue of $22.2 billion. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion. CEO Hock Tan guided for $56 billion in full-year FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue and reiterated a fiscal FY2027 target exceeding $100 billion, driven by demand from six custom-silicon customers including Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, and Anthropic. The $0.65 quarterly cash dividend — 14 consecutive years of increases — is payable tomorrow (June 30).

items.headline: Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational — enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute through 2028
items.detail: The Apollo and Blackstone-led $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform is operational, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026. Broadcom's Tomahawk 6 (Davisson) — the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics — is the networking layer for the next AI compute generation.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR trades at approximately $112–114, recovering from the 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25, though still roughly 35% below YTD highs. London's Metropolitan Police were granted a 12-month pilot extension on their Palantir AI deployment after a £50M contract was blocked. Wedbush set a $230 price target (roughly 100% upside). Q1 2026 revenue: $1.632B (+84.7% YoY) — highest growth rate in company history. Treasury contract, Army $10B, and DHS $1B anchor federal expansion.

items.headline: UK Met Police granted 12-month pilot extension after £50M contract blocked; Palantir threatens court challenge
items.detail: London's Metropolitan Police were granted a 12-month extension to their Palantir AI pilot on June 25 while a new procurement process runs, following Sadiq Khan's office blocking a £50 million contract over a 'clear and serious breach' of procurement rules. Palantir has threatened a court challenge over the blocked contract. PLTR fell 3.08% on June 12 when the contract block was first announced but has partially recovered.

items.headline: Wedbush $230 price target (100% upside); PLTR recovering from 52-week low; Q1 revenue $1.632B +84.7% YoY
items.detail: Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives reiterated a bullish Palantir outlook on June 24, setting a $230 12-month price target — implying nearly 100% upside from the stock's recent level around $112–114. Q1 2026 results posted $1.632 billion revenue (+84.7% YoY), the highest growth rate in company history. U.S. commercial revenue surged 133% to $595 million. Management raised full-year guidance to approximately 71% growth — a 10-point jump from prior guidance.

items.headline: Treasury contract (June 23) joins Army $10B and DHS $1B; Mythos restoration improves defense pipeline outlook
items.detail: Palantir won a U.S. Treasury Department contract on June 23 for a common API layer supporting developer platforms, workflow automation, and data analytics. 2026 federal contract wins include: $10B 10-year Army enterprise AI services, $1B 5-year DHS AI platform, $300M USDA deal (April), and the new Treasury contract. Mythos 5's partial restoration to critical infrastructure and government operators on June 26 improves the outlook for the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 with a fully rebuilt Gemini-powered Siri AI has been in developer beta since WWDC June 8; public beta expected mid-July with GA in September 2026. OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice over perceived lack of ChatGPT placement. Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option. Gemini 3.5 Pro's likely July delay (deadline tomorrow at ~50% odds) adds delivery risk to Apple's Gemini dependency.

items.headline: iOS 27 Gemini-powered Siri AI in developer beta since WWDC June 8; public beta mid-July; GA September 2026
items.detail: At WWDC June 8, Apple unveiled iOS 27 with a fully rebuilt Siri AI powered by Google's Gemini model, plus next-generation Apple Intelligence with personal context AI and Visual Intelligence. Siri AI features a dedicated standalone app, Dynamic Island integration, full on-screen awareness, and multi-step task execution via a three-tier system: on-device, Private Cloud Compute, and Gemini for complex tasks. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) is disabled pending EU DMA regulatory clarity. GA: September 2026.

items.headline: OpenAI weighs formal breach-of-contract notice over Siri placement; Apple evaluating Mistral as additional model
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the new Siri AI, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of June 29. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini, suggesting ongoing hedging across providers. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions remain confirmed dead.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro June 30 deadline 1 day away; July delay adds new delivery risk to Apple's Gemini dependency
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro — Apple's likely next Siri AI upgrade path — faces its June 30 self-imposed deadline tomorrow at roughly 50% prediction market odds. Multiple outlets report insider signals pointing to a July slip, coinciding with the DeepMind talent exodus (five senior researchers departed in one week). A delayed or underperforming Gemini 3.5 Pro directly constrains the capability ceiling of Apple's Gemini-powered Siri AI ahead of the September 2026 general availability launch.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: MI450 sampling is confirmed to lead customers OpenAI and Meta; Oracle announced a 50,000-unit MI450 deployment for the first publicly available AI supercluster in Q3 2026. MI450 specs: 432GB HBM4 memory, 20 TB/s bandwidth, 40 PFLOPs FP4 per GPU. Customer engagement on MI500 is underway for 2027. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 at Moscone Center; Q2 2026 results report August 4.

items.headline: Oracle deploys 50,000 MI450 GPUs for public AI supercluster Q3 2026; MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta
items.detail: Oracle announced a 50,000-unit AMD Instinct MI450 GPU deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, with initial deployment in Q3 2026 and planned 2027 expansion. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI (6GW partnership, beginning with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/6GW). MI450 features 432GB HBM4 memory, 20 TB/s bandwidth, 40 PFLOPs FP4, and 20 PFLOPs FP8 per GPU. Helios rack systems remain on H2 2026 schedule.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22–23 at Moscone Center; MI500 engagement underway; Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22–23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — is expected to formally launch MI450, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6) CPUs, confirm Helios rack-scale systems, and offer a first look at the MI500 series targeting 2027. AMD CEO confirmed active customer engagement on MI500, noting that the largest AI deployments are increasingly for inference at scale. MI450 demand exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections due to OAM-based deployments. Q2 2026 financial results: August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: CoreWeave powers Perplexity's inference workloads on dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters under a multi-year agreement. CEO Aravind Srinivas maintains a 2028 IPO target. The Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac and iOS, reaching #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of the iOS launch. Perplexity Computer runs as native side panels in Microsoft 365 apps. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ MAU; $20B valuation; $750M Azure deal.

items.headline: CoreWeave multi-year inference partnership powers Perplexity on dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters
items.detail: CoreWeave announced a multi-year strategic partnership with Perplexity to support its inference workloads on dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72-powered clusters on CoreWeave Cloud. The agreement ensures Perplexity's infrastructure keeps pace with rapid growth and the sophisticated requirements of the Sonar and Search API ecosystem. CoreWeave will integrate NVIDIA Rubin-based systems beginning H2 2026, positioning the partnership for next-generation model serving.

items.headline: 2028 IPO target firm; Comet browser #3 App Store; Perplexity Computer in Microsoft 365; $500M ARR; $20B valuation
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target, explicitly not accelerating despite Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead. The Comet AI browser reached #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of iOS launch and is globally available on Mac and iOS. Perplexity Computer runs as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ MAU; $20B valuation; $750M Azure deal.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CRWV closed at approximately $99.35 on June 29 (range $93.50–$99.35), up from $96.09 June 28, as Russell 3000 inclusion (effective June 26) continues to drive passive-fund buying. Vanguard increased its stake by 275.6% to nearly 28 million shares (~$2B). 37 analysts average 'Buy' with a $143.41 price target. Contracted backlog approaching $131 billion; 2026 capex floor $31 billion. The $3.1B HPC loan closed. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are customers.

items.headline: Russell 3000 effective June 26 — CRWV ~$99.35 on June 29; Vanguard +275.6% stake; analyst avg target $143.41
items.detail: CoreWeave's Russell 3000 inclusion became effective after the June 26 market close, triggering mandatory passive-fund buying. Vanguard increased its stake by 275.6% to nearly 28 million shares (~$2 billion) and PNC Financial Services raised its stake by 248.9%. CRWV closed at approximately $99.35 on June 29 (range $93.50–$99.35). Deutsche Bank maintained a Buy with a $135 price target; 37 analysts average 'Buy' with a $143.41 12-month price target.

items.headline: $3.1B HPC loan closed; backlog approaching $131B; 9 of top 10 AI labs on platform; Perplexity GB200 NVL72 partnership
items.detail: CoreWeave closed a $3.1 billion delayed draw term loan facility backed by its HPC infrastructure — the first public HPC infrastructure-backed financing structure for the AI cloud sector. Contracted backlog is approaching $131 billion, driven by Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout for Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers. The Perplexity multi-year partnership adds dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters for AI search inference. 2026 capex floor: $31 billion.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs) has its end-of-June opening window closing tomorrow (June 30). OCR 4 launched June 23 with 170-language coverage, 72% average win rate over competitors, and $4/1K pages API pricing. Mistral is in funding discussions at approximately €20B ($23.2B) valuation targeting a $3.5B raise. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral for future Siri/iOS integration.

items.headline: Paris data center end-of-June target closes tomorrow — 44MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs operated by Eclairion
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris — 44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems providing approximately 276 exaflops of FP4 compute, operated by Eclairion — has its end-of-June opening target closing tomorrow (June 30). No official opening announcement has been made as of June 29. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. The Bruyères-le-Châtel facility is the flagship of Mistral's planned 200 MW European compute roadmap.

items.headline: OCR 4 launched June 23 — 170 languages, 72% win rate, $4/1K pages API; runs on-premise for regulated enterprises
items.detail: Mistral AI released OCR 4 on June 23, a document intelligence model covering 170 languages that returns paragraph-level bounding boxes alongside extracted text, and deploys as a single container for organizations that cannot route sensitive documents to third-party cloud APIs. Independent annotators prefer OCR 4 over every leading OCR and document-AI system tested, with win rates averaging 72%, alongside the top overall score on OlmOCRBench (85.20). Available via API at $4 per 1,000 pages or via Mistral Studio's Document AI at $5 per 1,000 pages.

items.headline: Funding discussions at ~€20B ($23.2B) valuation; $3.5B raise target; CEO targets €1B 2026 revenue; Apple evaluating Mistral
items.detail: Mistral AI is in discussions with investors about raising approximately €3 billion ($3.5 billion) at a potential valuation near €20 billion ($23.2 billion), per Bloomberg. CEO Arthur Mensch targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue, up from €200 million in 2025. An $830M debt facility from Bpifrance and consortium banks funds the current data center buildout. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral for future Siri/iOS integration. Mistral launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20) with NVIDIA.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: The Cybercab robotaxi fleet has approximately 59–100+ units operational across Austin metro, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area, facing criticism for long waits and stalled rides. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont; V3 full-body production targets this summer. The Terafab initiative targets in-house AI5 chip production. Giga Texas North is targeting 10 million robots per year. Elon Musk told investors to be 'really patient' on ramp timelines.

items.headline: Cybercab fleet ~59–100+ units across four markets; EPA confirms 418-mile unadjusted range; Musk urges patience
items.detail: Tesla's Cybercab fleet has approximately 59 to 100+ units operating across the full Austin metro area, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. The service faces criticism for long passenger wait times and stalled rides. EPA filing (June 19) confirmed production Cybercab specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted), 3,113 lb curb weight. Elon Musk told investors June 20 to be 'really patient' with the ramp while robocar infrastructure expands in Texas.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 in mass production at Fremont; V3 full-body production targets summer 2026; Terafab AI5 chip initiative
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot is in mass production at Fremont, with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. Tesla's Terafab initiative targets in-house semiconductor fabrication for the AI5 chip powering Optimus Gen 3's onboard AI, FSD, and Cybercab systems. The Gigafactory Texas North Campus expansion (5.2 million square feet) targets 10 million robots per year long term. FSD v14.3 is deployed with 20% reduced inference latency.

ipoTracker.status: Fable 5 suspended Day 17; Mythos 5 partially restored June 26; Fable restoration 'as soon as this coming week' per Axios; AI for Science event tomorrow (June 30); $47B ARR run rate; first operating profit ~$559M Q2; $30B+ Series H closing at >$900B valuation; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO at a projected ~$1.1 trillion first-day market cap. The company is closing a Series H round exceeding $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion. Anthropic is tracking toward its first operating profit of approximately $559 million in Q2 2026 at a $47 billion annualized revenue run rate. As of June 29 (Day 17), Fable 5 remains suspended for all general users. Mythos 5 was partially restored June 26 to approximately 100 trusted companies and federal agencies. Axios reported the Trump administration is close to allowing Fable 5 access 'as soon as this coming week,' pending Pentagon and NSA sign-off. AI for Science event is tomorrow (June 30, 10 AM PST) featuring Nobel laureate John Jumper.
ipoTracker.note: AI for Science event tomorrow is the next public milestone. Fable 5 restoration this coming week is the key operating milestone. $47B ARR and first operating profitability trajectory strengthen the IPO case.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic IPO: Everything You Need to Know

ipoTracker.status: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna launched June 26 under government restrictions; IPO leaning 2027; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share 46.4% (first below 50%); Ona acquired June 21
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 8, 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness concerns, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility dynamics. GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna launched June 26 in limited preview under U.S. government-imposed access restrictions. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe June 12. ChatGPT's global market share fell to 46.4% — first time below 50%, with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 government restrictions and the 42-state AG probe are the dominant pre-IPO risk factors. EU AI Act chatbot enforcement begins August 2, 2026.

CBRS closed at $168.52 on June 25 (-7.61%) on forward gross margin compression guidance.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) Stock Jumps on Russell 3000 Entry and Fresh Analyst Upgrades

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed partnership; Paris data center end-of-June target closes tomorrow; Apple reportedly evaluating Mistral; Mistral Compute launched at VivaTech; OCR 4 live; funding talks at €20B
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20). Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 systems) has its end-of-June opening target tomorrow. Mistral OCR 4 launched June 23. Funding talks at ~€20B ($23.2B) valuation support further scale.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Paris data center opening window closes tomorrow (June 30). Apple's reported Mistral evaluation is a potential enterprise distribution catalyst. OCR 4 launch strengthens Mistral's enterprise position.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute contract — Colossus 2 data center access
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $6.3B deal — $150M/month starts July 1 (2 days away); Colossus 2 dedicated to Reflection AI
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI), providing access to NVIDIA GB300 GPUs via the Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. Reflection pays $150 million per month starting July 1 (2 days away) through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Combined with Colossus 1 rented to Anthropic, both Colossus facilities are committed to third parties.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CNBC reporting
partnershipTracker.note: July 1 start date is 2 days away. Grok 4.5 entering private beta at SpaceX alongside both Colossus facilities committed to third parties raises questions about Grok AI's own future dedicated compute access.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX AI Colossus data center signs deal with Reflection AI

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Operational — $35B capital solution; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute; Jalapeño chip reinforces Broadcom's position; dividend payable tomorrow (June 30)
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform is operational since June 9. The OpenAI-Broadcom Jalapeño chip (June 24) confirms OpenAI as Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer. Broadcom's $0.65 quarterly dividend is payable tomorrow (June 30). FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target reinforces platform scale.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: Broadcom dividend payable tomorrow (June 30). FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target confirms XPU platform is on a significant long-term growth trajectory.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom reveal Jalapeno, first AI chip in partnership
